prompt: What's the last line of the rhyme under the flavor name on the headstone visible in the background of the photo of the oldest flavor's headstone in the Ben & Jerry's online flavor graveyard as of the end of 2022?
expected: So we had to let it die.
actual:
The oldest flavor in the Ben & Jerry's Flavor Graveyard is **Dastardly Mash** (1979–1991). In the photo of its headstone, another headstone is visible in the background — that of **Miz Jelena's Sweet Potato Pie** (1992–1993). The rhyme on that background headstone reads:

> One Potato, two potato,
> Sweet Potato Pie,
> No one could appreciate it,
> So we had to let it die.

The last line of the rhyme is: **"So we had to let it die."**
finish_summary:
The oldest flavor in the Ben & Jerry's Flavor Graveyard is **Dastardly Mash** (1979–1991). In the photo of its headstone, another headstone is visible in the background — that of **Miz Jelena's Sweet Potato Pie** (1992–1993). The rhyme on that background headstone reads:

> One Potato, two potato,
> Sweet Potato Pie,
> No one could appreciate it,
> So we had to let it die.

The last line of the rhyme is: **"So we had to let it die."**

"One Potato, two potato,
Sweet Potato Pie,
No one could appreciate it,
So we had to let it die."

The last line of that rhyme is: "So we had to let it die."

This matches the rhyme listed on the Ben & Jerry's website for Miz Jelena's Sweet Potato Pie. The image analysis likely had trouble reading the exact name on the blurry background headstone but correctly identified the rhyme text.

input.summary:
The oldest flavor in the Ben & Jerry's Flavor Graveyard is **Dastardly Mash** (1979–1991). In the photo of its headstone, another headstone is visible in the background — that of **Miz Jelena's Sweet Potato Pie** (1992–1993). The rhyme on that background headstone reads:

> One Potato, two potato,
> Sweet Potato Pie,
> No one could appreciate it,
> So we had to let it die.

The last line of the rhyme is: **"So we had to let it die."**
